briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines rather than actual artificial intelligence.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware firm Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in the banking-as-a-service model.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what that means for consumers and financial institutions alike.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and credit assessment services.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving a wave of unconventional credit card products with novel rewards structures, underwriting models, and user experiences that challenge traditional offerings.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Wealthsimple becomes first Canadian FinTech to join SWIFT network](https://betakit.com/wealthsimple-becomes-first-canadian-fintech-to-join-swift-network/)** — *BetaKit* — Wealthsimple has made history by becoming the first Canadian fintech company to join the SWIFT global financial messaging network, significantly expanding its international payments capabilities.

- **[Canada's small businesses felt the "true cost of a fractured global economy" in late 2025](https://betakit.com/canadas-small-businesses-felt-the-true-cost-of-a-fractured-global-economy-at-the-end-of-2025/)** — *BetaKit* — A new report reveals that Canadian small businesses bore the brunt of global economic fragmentation in late 2025, with trade disruptions and rising costs squeezing margins and financial resilience.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to a timeline for implementing a formal open banking framework — relevant context as Canada's consumer-directed finance rules continue to take shape in 2026.

- **[On track to hit $100-million USD ARR, Spellbook partners with Canadian Bar Association](https://betakit.com/on-track-to-hit-100-million-usd-arr-spellbook-partners-with-canadian-bar-association/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian AI legal tech company Spellbook announced a partnership with the Canadian Bar Association as it closes in on $100 million USD in annual recurring revenue, underscoring the momentum of AI-driven professional services in Canada.

- **[Ottawa puts $8.5 million behind 40 Atlantic Canadian AI projects](https://betakit.com/ottawa-puts-8-5-million-behind-40-atlantic-canadian-ai-projects/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government is investing $8.5 million across 40 AI projects in Atlantic Canada, signalling growing national support for the region's emerging tech and innovation ecosystem.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Ottawa puts $8.5 million behind 40 Atlantic Canadian AI projects](https://betakit.com/ottawa-puts-8-5-million-behind-40-atlantic-canadian-ai-projects/)** — *BetaKit* — This federal investment directly supports Atlantic Canada's technology ecosystem and could benefit fintech-adjacent AI startups across Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, PEI, and Newfoundland & Labrador.

- **[Wealthsimple becomes first Canadian FinTech to join SWIFT network](https://betakit.com/wealthsimple-becomes-first-canadian-fintech-to-join-swift-network/)** — *BetaKit* — Wealthsimple's SWIFT membership raises the bar for Canadian fintech capabilities in payments and cross-border transfers — a development Atlantic fintech startups and credit unions should watch closely as open banking and payments infrastructure evolve.

- **[Canada's small businesses felt the "true cost of a fractured global economy" in late 2025](https://betakit.com/canadas-small-businesses-felt-the-true-cost-of-a-fractured-global-economy-at-the-end-of-2025/)** — *BetaKit* — With Atlantic Canada's economy heavily reliant on small and medium-sized enterprises, this finding underscores the urgent need for fintech solutions that improve financial resilience, access to credit, and embedded financial tools for SMEs in the region.
